bullet_summary: He said America's biggest adversaries are not overseas, but are on Capitol Hill
A top Pentagon official said the cost of the ongoing U

items.title: Iran war has cost $25 billion to date, defense official says
items.standfirst: The war with Iran is now in its ninth week, and Congress is concerned about the reduction of global munition stockpiles and the ability to restock them.
items.content: The war with Iran is now in its ninth week, and Congress is concerned about the reduction of global munition stockpiles and the ability to restock them.

items.title: Hegseth takes aim at Iran war critics during House testimony
items.standfirst: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth answered questions under oath in his first testimony since the war with Iran began. He said America's biggest adversaries are not overseas, but are on Capitol Hill. Caitlin Huey-Burns has more details.
items.content: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth answered questions under oath in his first testimony since the war with Iran began. He said America's biggest adversaries are not overseas, but are on Capitol Hill. Caitlin Huey-Burns has more details.

items.title: Lawmakers press Pete Hegseth about Kuwait strike in Iran war that killed 6 U.S. troops
items.standfirst: Top Pentagon leaders, including Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, testified before the House Armed Services Committee on Wednesday, facing tough questions about the war with Iran. CBS News' Taurean Small and Aaron Navarro have more details.
items.content: Top Pentagon leaders, including Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, testified before the House Armed Services Committee on Wednesday, facing tough questions about the war with Iran. CBS News' Taurean Small and Aaron Navarro have more details.

items.title: Fetterman calls on Democrats to unify behind Iran war
items.standfirst: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified Wednesday in front of lawmakers on Capitol Hill for the first time since the start of the war in Iran. Democratic Sen. John Fetterman of Pennsylvania joins "The Takeout" to discuss Hegseth's testimony.
items.content: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified Wednesday in front of lawmakers on Capitol Hill for the first time since the start of the war in Iran. Democratic Sen. John Fetterman of Pennsylvania joins "The Takeout" to discuss Hegseth's testimony.

items.title: DOD officials say Iran war has cost $25 billion so far during Congressional grilling
items.standfirst: The Pentagon says that the cost of the war with Iran is estimated to be some $25 billion. Defense officials were appearing on the Hill for budget discussions.
items.content: The Pentagon says that the cost of the war with Iran is estimated to be some $25 billion. Defense officials were appearing on the Hill for budget discussions.

items.title: The Iran war now has a price tag ($25 billion), but still no end date
items.standfirst: The Pentagon estimates the war has cost $25 billion over the past two months. In congressional testimony, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth did not say when the war might end.
items.content: The Pentagon estimates the war has cost $25 billion over the past two months. In congressional testimony, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth did not say when the war might end. (Image credit: Rod Lamkey Jr.)

items.title: US lawmakers question Pete Hegseth over budget for war on Iran
items.standfirst: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified before Congress on the Pentagon’s budget for the US-Israeli war on Iran.
items.content: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified before Congress on the Pentagon’s budget for the US-Israeli war on Iran.

items.title: Iran war has cost the U.S. $25 billion so far, Pentagon official says
items.standfirst: A top Pentagon official said the cost of the ongoing U.S. war with Iran is estimated to be $25 billion so far, as the conflict nears a 60-day mark.
items.content: A top Pentagon official said the cost of the ongoing U.S. war with Iran is estimated to be $25 billion so far, as the conflict nears a 60-day mark.

items.title: REPLAY: US lawmakers grill Pentagon chief Hegseth over Iran war
items.standfirst: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is facing questioning from lawmakers for the first time since US President Donald Trump launched the war against Iran, which Democrats have contested as a costly conflict of choice waged without congressional approval. Watch our replay.
items.content: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is facing questioning from lawmakers for the first time since US President Donald Trump launched the war against Iran, which Democrats have contested as a costly conflict of choice waged without congressional approval. Watch our replay.

items.content: Former FBI Director James Comey turned himself in on Wednesday after he was indicted and charged with threatening the life of President Trump last year.

items.title: James Comey makes first court appearance for charges in connection with social media post
items.standfirst: Former FBI Director James Comey made his first court appearance in Virginia on Wednesday, charged with threatening President Trump. A grand jury indicted Comey yesterday over a social media post he made last year. CBS News Department of Justice reporter Jake Rosen has more.
items.content: Former FBI Director James Comey made his first court appearance in Virginia on Wednesday, charged with threatening President Trump. A grand jury indicted Comey yesterday over a social media post he made last year. CBS News Department of Justice reporter Jake Rosen has more.

items.content: Prosecutors say a 2025 seashell photo posted by the former FBI director was a call for violence against Trump.

items.standfirst: Comey turned himself in on two charges, threatening the US president's life and transmitting threats across state lines.
items.content: Comey turned himself in on two charges, threatening the US president's life and transmitting threats across state lines.

items.title: '8647' got James Comey indicted. What exactly does it mean?
items.standfirst: A grand jury charged Comey with threatening Trump's life through his since-deleted 2025 post of seashells forming "8647." Trump is the 47th president, and the term "86" has a few possible meanings.

”"A 45-year-old man, who is a British national, born in Somalia, was arrested on suspicion of attempted murder," Metropolitan Police said in a statement

items.title: Two Jewish men stabbed in London
items.standfirst: A 45-year-old suspect has been arrested after stabbing two Jewish men in North London. New video shows the dramatic moment officers took the suspect down. NBC News’ Daniele Hamamdjian reports from the scene.
items.content: A 45-year-old suspect has been arrested after stabbing two Jewish men in North London. New video shows the dramatic moment officers took the suspect down. NBC News’ Daniele Hamamdjian reports from the scene.

items.title: What we know about the Golders Green stabbings
items.standfirst: Two Jewish men have been stabbed in Golders Green, north London, an attack police have declared a terrorist incident.
items.content: Two Jewish men have been stabbed in Golders Green, north London, an attack police have declared a terrorist incident.

items.title: Police declare terrorist incident after two Jewish men stabbed in London
items.standfirst: A 45-year-old man has been arrested on suspicion of attempted murder, the Met Police says.
items.content: A 45-year-old man has been arrested on suspicion of attempted murder, the Met Police says.

items.title: Watch: Met Police body-worn footage of Golders Green arrest
items.standfirst: Footage shows the moment a man was arrested after two Jewish men were stabbed in north London.
items.content: Footage shows the moment a man was arrested after two Jewish men were stabbed in north London.

items.title: Two Jewish men stabbed in London ‘terrorist attack’
items.standfirst: Two Jewish men were stabbed in north London, after a man armed with a knife was seen running through the area.
items.content: Two Jewish men were stabbed in north London, after a man armed with a knife was seen running through the area.

items.title: Two wounded as knife attacker targets London’s Jewish community
items.standfirst: U.K. police declared a stabbing attack that injured two Jewish people in London was a "terrorist incident." A 45-year-old man with a history of mental health issues stabbed and seriously wounded two Jewish men in Golders Green, North London.
items.content: U.K. police declared a stabbing attack that injured two Jewish people in London was a "terrorist incident." A 45-year-old man with a history of mental health issues stabbed and seriously wounded two Jewish men in Golders Green, North London.

items.title: London police say stabbing of 2 Jewish men is declared a terrorist incident
items.standfirst: Two Jewish men were stabbed and injured in a London street on Wednesday, in what police called an act of terrorism. Police arrested a 45-year-old man on suspicion of attempted murder in the city's latest antisemitic attack. The Metropolitan Police said the attack in the Golders Green area left two men, aged 34 and 76, hospitalized with knife wounds.
items.content: Two Jewish men were stabbed and injured in a London street on Wednesday, in what police called an act of terrorism. Police arrested a 45-year-old man on suspicion of attempted murder in the city's latest antisemitic attack. The Metropolitan Police said the attack in the Golders Green area left two men, aged 34 and 76, hospitalized with knife wounds.

items.title: Stabbing of two Jewish people in London declared a terrorist incident
items.standfirst: LONDON — Metropolitan Police say they have arrested a 45-year-old man in connection with a stabbing attack that injured two Jewish people in London which they called a “terrorist incident.”"A 45-year-old man, who is a British national, born in Somalia, was arrested on suspicion of attempted murder," Metropolitan Police said in a statement.
items.content: LONDON — Metropolitan Police say they have arrested a 45-year-old man in connection with a stabbing attack that injured two Jewish people in London which they called a “terrorist incident.”"A 45-year-old man, who is a British national, born in Somalia, was arrested on suspicion of attempted murder," Metropolitan Police said in a statement.

items.title: Jerome Powell to stay on Fed board after central bank holds rates steady in defiance of Trump
items.standfirst: Chair planned to exit after inquiry into building renovations but will now oversee ‘remaining steps in the process’Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe US Federal Reserve chair, Jerome Powell, will remain on the central bank’s rate-setting board after his term as chair ends in May, a contentious move that signals continued uncertainty at the Fed.Powell made the announcement after the Fed board on Wednesday left interest rates unchanged for the third time this year, despite Donald Trump’s continued demands for rate cuts. Continue reading...

items.title: FCC orders review of ABC licenses after Jimmy Kimmel’s Melania Trump joke
items.standfirst: Eight local broadcasting licenses under review after White House launched attack on late-night host over commentSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe US’s top media watchdog announced on Tuesday that it is accelerating the review of eight local broadcasting licenses used by ABC, in a move critics see as a clear example of political and regulatory retribution against a disfavored broadcaster.The Federal Communications Commission’s (FCC) announcement comes after the White House launched a full-on attack against the ABC’s late-night host, Jimmy Kimmel, over a joke he made last week about Melania Trump. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Florida approves US House map meant to boost Republicans in midterms
clusters.neutral_headline: Florida approves US House map meant to boost Republicans in midterms
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, Al Jazeera, NPR News

items.title: Florida approves US House map meant to boost Republicans in midterms
items.standfirst: Vote comes on same day the US supreme court rolls back a key provision of the Voting Rights ActThe Florida legislature approved a new congressional map intended to maximize Republicans’ advantage in the state as part of the national redistricting battle that Donald Trump launched before this year’s midterms.The vote came just two days after the governor, Ron DeSantis, unveiled his proposal and the same day the US supreme court rolled back a key provision of the Voting Rights Act. The decision could make it harder for Democrats to challenge Republican efforts to redraw congressional districts in ways that limit the influence of voters of color. Continue reading...
items.content: Vote comes on same day the US supreme court rolls back a key provision of the Voting Rights ActThe Florida legislature approved a new congressional map intended to maximize Republicans’ advantage in the state as part of the national redistricting battle that Donald Trump launched before this year’s midterms.The vote came just two days after the governor, Ron DeSantis, unveiled his proposal and the same day the US supreme court rolled back a key provision of the Voting Rights Act. The decision could make it harder for Democrats to challenge Republican efforts to redraw congressional districts in ways that limit the influence of voters of color. Continue reading...

items.content: Florida has become latest state to redraw map, as national electoral landscape transforms before midterm elections.

items.title: Florida lawmakers pass a voting map that could help Republicans flip 4 House seats
items.standfirst: The map drawn by Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis boosts President Trump's effort to reshape voting before the midterm elections. The GOP likely holds a slight edge over Democrats in redistricting now.
items.content: The map drawn by Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis boosts President Trump's effort to reshape voting before the midterm elections. The GOP likely holds a slight edge over Democrats in redistricting now.(Image credit: Mike Stewart)

clusters.title: VAR denies Arsenal late penalty in draw against Atletico
clusters.neutral_headline: Arsenal cruelly denied penalty by VAR in Champions League draw at Atletico
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC Top Stories, France24, Al Jazeera

items.title: VAR denies Arsenal late penalty in draw against Atletico
items.standfirst: Viktor Gyokeres puts Arsenal ahead from the spot before an equalising penalty from Atletico Madrid's Julian Alvarez in the second half. A third penalty call for the Gunners is overturned by VAR which ensures the first leg of their Champions League semi-final ends in a 1-1 draw.
items.content: Viktor Gyokeres puts Arsenal ahead from the spot before an equalising penalty from Atletico Madrid's Julian Alvarez in the second half. A third penalty call for the Gunners is overturned by VAR which ensures the first leg of their Champions League semi-final ends in a 1-1 draw.

items.title: Arsenal, Atletico Madrid share draw after penalty drama in Champions League semi-final
items.standfirst: Julian Alvarez scored a penalty as Atletico Madrid held Arsenal to a 1-1 draw in their UEFA Champions League semi-final first leg on Wednesday. Viktor Gyokeres had put Arsenal ahead from the spot before Alvarez equalised, while a late penalty call for Arsenal was overturned after a VAR review.
items.content: Julian Alvarez scored a penalty as Atletico Madrid held Arsenal to a 1-1 draw in their UEFA Champions League semi-final first leg on Wednesday. Viktor Gyokeres had put Arsenal ahead from the spot before Alvarez equalised, while a late penalty call for Arsenal was overturned after a VAR review.

items.title: Arsenal cruelly denied penalty by VAR in Champions League draw at Atletico
items.standfirst: Arsenal scored one penalty, but VAR controversially denied them another in a 1-1 semifinal draw at Atletico Madrid.
items.content: Arsenal scored one penalty, but VAR controversially denied them another in a 1-1 semifinal draw at Atletico Madrid.

items.title: South Africa deports Mugabe’s son for unrelated offences after employee shot at family home
items.standfirst: Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe also fined after pleading guilty to immigration and firearms-related offencesTwo months after an employee was shot in the back at the Mugabe family home in a wealthy suburb of Johannesburg, a South African court has fined and ordered the deportation of Robert Mugabe’s youngest son over two unrelated charges.Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe, 28, and his cousin Tobias Mugabe Matonhodze, 33, were initially both charged with attempted murder after the incident on 19 February. clusters.title: Maryland becomes first state to ban surveillance pricing in grocery stores
clusters.neutral_headline: Maryland becomes first state to ban surveillance pricing in grocery stores
bullet_summary: Maryland is pushing forward because we are going to protect our people,” Moore said at the bill signing ceremony
Reported by Guardian World, Reuters Top News

items.title: Maryland becomes first state to ban surveillance pricing in grocery stores
items.standfirst: Critics say Maryland’s new law banning rapidly change product costs based on consumer data is full of carveoutsMaryland has become the first state in the US to ban surveillance pricing in grocery stores.Maryland’s law bans grocers and third-party delivery services from using a person’s personal data to set higher prices. Wes Moore, the governor, signed the measure into law on Tuesday. “At a time when technology can predict what we need, when we need it, when we’ll pay for it and also – when we’ll pay more for it, and at a time when we’re watching how big companies are then using these analytics against us to make record profits, Maryland is not just pushing back. Maryland is pushing forward because we are going to protect our people,” Moore said at the bill signing ceremony. Continue reading...
items.content: Critics say Maryland’s new law banning rapidly change product costs based on consumer data is full of carveoutsMaryland has become the first state in the US to ban surveillance pricing in grocery stores.Maryland’s law bans grocers and third-party delivery services from using a person’s personal data to set higher prices. Wes Moore, the governor, signed the measure into law on Tuesday. “At a time when technology can predict what we need, when we need it, when we’ll pay for it and also – when we’ll pay more for it, and at a time when we’re watching how big companies are then using these analytics against us to make record profits, Maryland is not just pushing back. Maryland is pushing forward because we are going to protect our people,” Moore said at the bill signing ceremony. Continue reading...

items.title: Maryland becomes first state to ban surveillance pricing in grocery stores
items.standfirst: Maryland is the first U.S. state to ban grocery stores and third-party delivery services from using personal data to set higher prices. The new law targets surveillance pricing, but critics say it includes broad exemptions for loyalty programs and promotions and weak enforcement provisions. [theguardian.com]
items.content: Maryland is the first U.S. state to ban grocery stores and third-party delivery services from using personal data to set higher prices. The new law targets surveillance pricing, but critics say it includes broad exemptions for loyalty programs and promotions and weak enforcement provisions. [theguardian.com]

bullet_summary: Reported by NPR News, Guardian World

items.title: Supreme Court appears to lean toward ending TPS for some migrants
items.standfirst: The U.S. Supreme Court appeared sympathetic to the Trump administration's move to end temporary protected status for Haitians and Syrians in the country.
items.content: The U.S. Supreme Court appeared sympathetic to the Trump administration's move to end temporary protected status for Haitians and Syrians in the country.(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)

items.title: US supreme court conservatives seem to favor ending TPS for Haitians and Syrians
items.standfirst: Nine justices were hearing Trump administration that it has authority to strip immigrants’ temporary protected status The US supreme court heard oral arguments on Wednesday over whether the Trump administration can strip the temporary protected status (TPS) of hundreds of thousands of immigrant Haitians and Syrians, under a program that has shielded them from deportation owing to safety concerns in their countries of origin.During the arguments, justices in the conservative-leaning majority appeared sympathetic to the Trump administration’s attempts to strip humanitarian protections for the Syrians and Haitians in this case. Continue reading...
items.content: Nine justices were hearing Trump administration that it has authority to strip immigrants’ temporary protected status The US supreme court heard oral arguments on Wednesday over whether the Trump administration can strip the temporary protected status (TPS) of hundreds of thousands of immigrant Haitians and Syrians, under a program that has shielded them from deportation owing to safety concerns in their countries of origin.During the arguments, justices in the conservative-leaning majority appeared sympathetic to the Trump administration’s attempts to strip humanitarian protections for the Syrians and Haitians in this case. Continue reading...

items.title: House extends a controversial spy tool, but Senate path is unclear ahead of deadline

items.content: The House has approved a three year extension of the surveillance program known as FISA Section 702. The bill now heads to the Senate, where it faces a difficult path to final passage.(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)

Reported by Guardian World, BBC Top Stories

items.title: Stephen Fry sues tech conference organisers for £100,000 over fall from stage
items.standfirst: Actor and presenter broke his hip, right leg, pelvis and ribs when he gave a talk at CogX festival at O2 Arena in 2023Stephen Fry is suing two companies that organised a tech conference where he was injured in 2023 after falling off the stage, high court documents show.The actor and presenter broke his hip and had multiple breaks in his right leg, pelvis and ribs when he attended the CogX festival at the O2 Arena, where he delivered a talk on artificial intelligence on 14 September 2023. Continue reading...
items.content: Actor and presenter broke his hip, right leg, pelvis and ribs when he gave a talk at CogX festival at O2 Arena in 2023Stephen Fry is suing two companies that organised a tech conference where he was injured in 2023 after falling off the stage, high court documents show.The actor and presenter broke his hip and had multiple breaks in his right leg, pelvis and ribs when he attended the CogX festival at the O2 Arena, where he delivered a talk on artificial intelligence on 14 September 2023. Continue reading...

items.title: Stephen Fry sues tech conference for up to £100,000 after falling off stage
items.standfirst: The star said he broke his leg, hip, pelvis and a "bunch of ribs" at the CogX convention in 2023.
items.content: The star said he broke his leg, hip, pelvis and a "bunch of ribs" at the CogX convention in 2023.

clusters.title: Mali’s Tuareg rebels say Russian fighters must withdraw from country
clusters.neutral_headline: Mali’s Tuareg rebels say Russian fighters must withdraw from country
bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, France24

items.title: Mali’s Tuareg rebels say Russian fighters must withdraw from country
items.standfirst: As military government battles to reassert control, spokesperson for Tuareg rebels says it will fall 'sooner or later'.
items.content: As military government battles to reassert control, spokesperson for Tuareg rebels says it will fall 'sooner or later'.

items.title: Tuareg rebels urge Russian forces to leave Mali
items.standfirst: Russian forces must withdraw from "all of Mali" and the ruling junta will "fall sooner or later", a spokesman for Tuareg rebels told AFP, after Islamist insurgents and separatists launched attacks destabilising the west African country. FRANCE 24's Juliette Brown reports.
items.content: Russian forces must withdraw from "all of Mali" and the ruling junta will "fall sooner or later", a spokesman for Tuareg rebels told AFP, after Islamist insurgents and separatists launched attacks destabilising the west African country. FRANCE 24's Juliette Brown reports.

clusters.title: Kim praises North Korean soldiers who blew themselves up to evade Ukraine capture
clusters.neutral_headline: Kim Jong Un praises troops who 'self-criticized' to avoid capture by Ukraine
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Kim praises North Korean soldiers who blew themselves up to evade Ukraine capture
items.standfirst: Leader mentions for first time lengths to which troops go to avoid falling into enemy hands while fighting for RussiaKim Jong-un has praised North Korean soldiers who blew themselves up with grenades in order to avoid capture while fighting Ukrainian forces in Russia’s western Kursk region, confirming the existence of the extreme battlefield policy.Mounting evidence, including from intelligence reports and testimonies of defectors, has indicated North Korean soldiers are explicitly told to resort to self-detonation or other forms of suicide to avoid falling into enemy hands. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Kim Jong Un praises troops who 'self-blasted' to avoid capture by Ukraine
items.standfirst: It confirms suspicions that North Korean soldiers are being told to detonate their grenades to kill themselves.
items.content: It confirms suspicions that North Korean soldiers are being told to detonate their grenades to kill themselves.
